title: Cambridge man who threw cat out of window sentenced for animal cruelty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jaydan Depina, 22, from Cambridge, sentenced to 90 days in prison and two years probation for animal cruelty.
Depina admitted to abusing his cat, Sage; the cat was thrown out of a window and suffered multiple injuries.
A Middlesex judge mandated mental health treatment and no contact with animals for Depina after his release.

title: Man killed on Boston highway after head-on crash with wrong-way driver
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 81-year-old wrong-way driver caused a fatal head-on crash on I-93 near Exit 15A in Boston.
The driver, Antone Carvalho, initially caused a collision involving an Audi and a family of four in a Honda.
The victim, a man from Haverhill in his 20s, died after Carvalho's SUV collided with his Chevrolet Cruze.

title: Jarren Duran had another confrontation with a fan at Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jarren Duran had an altercation with a fan during Red Sox's 4-1 win over the Yankees at Fenway Park.
The fan, wearing a Yankees hat, was escorted out after heckling Duran, according to interim manager Chad Tracy.
This incident marks the second confrontation this season for Duran, previously responding to heckling in a game against the Twins.

title: Brookline party store, Chinese restaurant both close after decades in business
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Golden Temple in Brookline closed after 66 years, known for its E Room bar and late-night dining.
Party Favors, a Brookline bakery and party store, will close on July 15 after 35 years in business.
Both businesses expressed gratitude to the community, citing customers' support but not specifying reasons for closing.

title: Celtics reportedly sign Ron Harper Jr. to 3-year deal
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Celtics signed Ron Harper Jr. to a three-year, $9 million contract, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
Boston declined Harper's $2.6 million team option for the 2026-27 season to extend his contract.
Harper averaged 4.0 points and 1.7 rebounds in 26 games during the 2025-26 season.

title: 2 JP residents bitten by raccoons in separate incidents, animal control says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people were bitten by raccoons in separate incidents in Jamaica Plain over the weekend, according to local authorities.
The victims were bitten at Jamaica Pond and near Kenton Street and were treated at area hospitals, officials reported.
Animal control officers have been unable to locate the raccoons; residents are advised to exercise caution around wild animals.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the Norway-France World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup group stage match at Boston's Gillette Stadium.
Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick, including the second fastest in World Cup history, for France.
Over 64,000 fans attended, with Norway supporters creating iconic World Cup scenes.

title: 5 prospects to watch during Bruins Development Camp
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Nearly 30 players, including many 2026 draft picks, will attend Boston Bruins' Development Camp at Warrior Ice Arena.
Dean Letourneau is noted for his potential with Boston, boasting size and scoring after a strong sophomore year at Boston College.
Swedish goaltender Max Lundgren is a key prospect following his MVP performance leading Merrimack to a Hockey East title.

title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eleven people were injured, one seriously, when a duck boat overturned on a ramp in Cambridge on Saturday.
The duck boat tipped over at 10 Museum Way after the tow rope snapped while being pulled from the Charles River.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations for the day and is collaborating with authorities to review the incident.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded two first-round picks for 24-year-old winger JJ Peterka from the Utah Mammoth.
Peterka, averaging 26 goals per season, adds speed and scoring depth to the Bruins' top-six lineup.
Boston seeks to revitalize their roster, focusing on youth and skill, following past draft capital forfeitures.
